---
title: "DESeq2_Clemson_output"
author: "Galen O'Shea-Stone"
date: "2025-06-30"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}



# Read in data
## FULL SET
#All.samples.gene.counts.renamed <- read.delim("redo.ensembl.stranded.counts.renamed.txt")

## SUBSET
All.samples.gene.counts.renamed <- read.delim("redo.ensembl.stranded.counts.renamed.subset.txt")

# Rename rows
Expression<-All.samples.gene.counts.renamed
row.names(Expression) = Expression$Geneid

# Verify columns in dataset
head(Expression)

# Add and load DESeq2 package
#if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#    install.packages("BiocManager")
#  BiocManager::install("AnnotationDbi")
#  BiocManager::install("org.Gg.eg.db")
  


#BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
library("DESeq2")

## FULL SET 
# verify all metrics are integers (ideally should be estimated read counts as per the DESeq2 manual)
#cts<-as.data.frame(Expression[,2:13])

## SUBSET 
# verify all metrics are integers (ideally should be estimated read counts as per the DESeq2 manual)
cts<-as.data.frame(Expression[,2:11])


for (i in 1:4){
  cts[,i]<-as.integer(cts[,i])
}

# match row name of new table to original dataset
row.names(cts) = Expression$Geneid

## FULL SET 
# make conditions table that includes treatment types. Here it is venom time (A or B). Could include a number of variables (like sequencing batch or sequence type)
#condition = c("experimental", "controlF","controlF","experimental","controlP","experimental", "experimental", "controlF", "controlP", "controlF", "controlP", "controlP")
#type = c("paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end","paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end")

## SUBSET
condition = c("controlF","controlF","experimental","controlP","experimental", "experimental", "controlP", "controlF", "controlP", "controlP")
type = c("paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end","paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end", "paired-end")

coldata <- cbind(condition,type)
row.names(coldata) = colnames(cts)
colnames(coldata) = c("condition","type")

# Rownames of conditions need to match column names of cts, so verify this with the statement below
# R should return the value TRUE if they match
all(rownames(coldata) == colnames(cts))

##Set up input matrix for DESeq2. This is where the experimental design is specified
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = cts, colData = coldata, design = ~ condition)


##Remove genes with all zeros
dds <- dds[rowSums(assay(dds)) > 0, ]
dim(dds)


## Run analysis
dds <- DESeq(dds)

## Check comparisons
resultsNames(dds)

## Seeing it used ControlF as the control, generate a second model using controlP as the control
dds_P<-dds
dds_P$condition<-relevel(dds_P$condition, ref="controlP")
dds_P<-DESeq(dds_P)
resultsNames(dds_P)


## log transform
rld <- rlogTransformation(dds)
rld_P<- rlogTransformation(dds_P)

## plot
pdf("DESeq2.pca.plot.pdf")
## FULL SET
#plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "condition", ntop=25206)
## SUBSET
plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "condition", ntop=24721)

dev.off()

# ## something else with plots
 pca_data <- plotPCA(rld, intgroup = "condition", returnData = TRUE, ntop=24721)

# ggplot(pca_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = condition, label = name)) +
#   geom_point() +
#   geom_text()

## FULL Data
# pdf("DESeq2.pca.plot.draft.pdf")
# ggplot(pca_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = condition, label = c( "14S1", "16F", "17F", "18S2", "1P", "20S1", "20S3", "2F", "2P", "3F","4P" ,"9P"))) +
#   geom_point() +
#   geom_text()
# dev.off()


## SUBSET
 pdf("DESeq2.pca.plot.names.pdf")
 ggplot(pca_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = condition, label = c( "16F", "17F", "18S2", "1P", "20S1", "20S3", "2P", "3F","4P" ,"9P"))) +
   geom_point() +
   geom_text()
 dev.off()


## inspect size
sizeFactors(dds)
sizeFactors(dds_P)

resultsNames(dds)
dds$condition


## TEST
res_test <- results(dds)
res_test


##Create and view table of results. Adjusted p-value will be the things to look at.
res <- results(dds,tidy = T)
#View(res)
res
write.csv(res, file="DESeq2_experimental_vs_controlF.csv")

## Run to verify comparison being output
#res_controlP_vs_controlF <- results(dds, name="condition_controlP_vs_controlF")
## Cleans up for output table
res_controlP_vs_controlF <- results(dds, name="condition_controlP_vs_controlF", tidy=T)

write.csv(res_controlP_vs_controlF, file="DESeq2_controlP_vs_controlF.csv")

resultsNames(dds_P)
## Verify comparison
#res_P<- results(dds_P)
#res_P

## Clean output for table
res_P<- results(dds_P,tidy=T)
write.csv(res_P, file="DESeq2_experimental_vs_controlP.csv")



# plot MDS
rld <- rlog(dds)
plotPCA(rld)









#==========Clusterprofiler
# Add and load associated package (ONly run the first time)
#if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#    install.packages("BiocManager")
#BiocManager::install("clusterProfiler")
#BiocManager::install("pathview")
#BiocManager::install("enrichplot")
#BiocManager::install("AnnotationHub")

##library("AnnotationDbi")
##library("org.Gg.eg.db")




library(clusterProfiler)
library(enrichplot)
# we use ggplot2 to add x axis labels (ex: ridgeplot)
library(ggplot2)

# SET THE DESIRED ORGANISM HERE
organism = "org.Gg.eg.db"

## Only need to install the first time
#BiocManager::install(organism, character.only = TRUE)

library(organism, character.only = TRUE)

## Prepare Input
## reading in data from deseq2
## Starting wtih experimental VS control F comparison
df = read.csv("DESeq2_experimental_vs_controlF.csv", header=TRUE)

head(df)
#   X                row   baseMean log2FoldChange     lfcSE      stat       pvalue         padj
# 1 1 ENSGALG00010000711  273.99625     -1.2848173 0.2461931 -5.218737 1.801470e-07 1.843511e-06
# 2 2 ENSGALG00010000715   64.23415     -1.1621565 0.4732293 -2.455800 1.405713e-02 4.595885e-02
# 3 3 ENSGALG00010000717 2262.62245      1.0244561 0.2262098  4.528787 5.932338e-06 4.628848e-05
# 4 4 ENSGALG00010000720 1897.17538      0.3672470 0.1939990  1.893035 5.835316e-02 1.459544e-01
# 5 5 ENSGALG00010000724  413.59747     -0.3563058 0.2305785 -1.545269 1.222812e-01 2.554213e-01
# 6 6 ENSGALG00010000729   93.42448     -2.7552271 0.3942623 -6.988309 2.782190e-12 5.414096e-11


## From DESeq2 - remove the first column of just numbers
df<-df[,-1]

head(df)
#                  row   baseMean log2FoldChange     lfcSE      stat       pvalue         padj
# 1 ENSGALG00010000711  273.99625     -1.2848173 0.2461931 -5.218737 1.801470e-07 1.843511e-06
# 2 ENSGALG00010000715   64.23415     -1.1621565 0.4732293 -2.455800 1.405713e-02 4.595885e-02
# 3 ENSGALG00010000717 2262.62245      1.0244561 0.2262098  4.528787 5.932338e-06 4.628848e-05
# 4 ENSGALG00010000720 1897.17538      0.3672470 0.1939990  1.893035 5.835316e-02 1.459544e-01
# 5 ENSGALG00010000724  413.59747     -0.3563058 0.2305785 -1.545269 1.222812e-01 2.554213e-01
# 6 ENSGALG00010000729   93.42448     -2.7552271 0.3942623 -6.988309 2.782190e-12 5.414096e-11


df$row <- mapIds(org.Gg.eg.db,
                     keys=row.names(df),
                     column="ENSEMBL",
                     keytype="ALIAS",
                     multiVals="first")






# we want the log2 fold change 
original_gene_list <- df$log2FoldChange

# name the vector
names(original_gene_list) <- df$row

# omit any NA values 
gene_list<-na.omit(original_gene_list)

# sort the list in decreasing order (required for clusterProfiler)
gene_list = sort(gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)

## To see the keyTypes available in the orgDB object
keytypes(org.Gg.eg.db)

## Definitions of keytypes
#?AnnotationDbi::ACCNUM


gse.take2 <- gseGO(geneList=gene_list, 
             ont ="ALL", 
             keyType = "ENSEMBL", 
             nPerm = 10000, 
             minGSSize = 3, 
             maxGSSize = 800, 
             pvalueCutoff = 0.05, 
             verbose = TRUE, 
             OrgDb = org.Gg.eg.db, 
             pAdjustMethod = "none")


require(DOSE)
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_dotplot.pdf")
dotplot(gse.take2, showCategory=10, split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
dev.off()

## Enrichment Map (NOT WORKING)
emapplot(gse.take2, showCategory = 10)

# categorySize can be either 'pvalue' or 'geneNum'
# Choosing a category to show
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_category_netplot.pdf")
cnetplot(gse.take2, categorySize="pvalue", foldChange=gene_list, showCategory = 3)
dev.off()

## Ridgeplot
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_ridgeplot.pdf")
ridgeplot(gse.take2) + labs(x = "enrichment distribution")
dev.off()

##GSEA plot
# Use the `Gene Set` param for the index in the title, and as the value for geneSetId
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_GSEA_plot.pdf")
gseaplot(gse.take2, by = "all", title = gse.take2$Description[1], geneSetID = 1)
dev.off()

##PUBMED trend of enriched terms (top hits)
terms <- gse.take2$Description[1:3]
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_PUBMED_trend_search.pdf")
pmcplot(terms, 2019:2024, proportion=FALSE)
dev.off()



library("biomaRt")
head(gene_list)
column_names <- colnames(gene_list)
head(column_names)



## KEGG GSEA
# Convert gene IDs for gseKEGG function
# We will lose some genes here because not all IDs will be converted
ids<-bitr(names(original_gene_list), fromType = "ENSEMBL", toType = "ENTREZID", OrgDb=organism)
# remove duplicate IDS (here I use "ENSEMBL", but it should be whatever was selected as keyType)
dedup_ids = ids[!duplicated(ids[c("ENTREZID")]),]
##dedup_dedup_ids = dedup_ids[!duplicated(dedup_ids[c("ENSEMBL")]),]



# Create a new dataframe df2 which has only the genes which were successfully mapped using the bitr function above
df2 = df[df$row %in% dedup_dedup_ids$ENSEMBL,]

# Create a new column in df2 with the corresponding ENTREZ IDs
df2$Y = dedup_dedup_ids$ENTREZID

# Create a vector of the gene unuiverse
kegg_gene_list <- df2$log2FoldChange

# Name vector with ENTREZ ids
names(kegg_gene_list) <- df2$Y

# omit any NA values 
kegg_gene_list<-na.omit(kegg_gene_list)

# sort the list in decreasing order (required for clusterProfiler)
kegg_gene_list = sort(kegg_gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)



## Check KEGG organism code
search_kegg_organism('gga', by='kegg_code')


## Create gseKEGG Object
kegg_organism = "gga"
kk2 <- gseKEGG(geneList     = kegg_gene_list,
               organism     = kegg_organism,
               minGSSize    = 3,
               maxGSSize    = 800,
               pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
               pAdjustMethod = "none",
               keyType       = "ncbi-geneid")



## dotplot
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_KEGG_dotplot.pdf")
dotplot(kk2, showCategory = 10, title = "Enriched Pathways" , split=".sign") + facet_grid(.~.sign)
dev.off()


## Enrichment Plot (failed again)
emapplot(kk2)

# categorySize can be either 'pvalue' or 'geneNum'
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_KEGG_category_plot.pdf")
cnetplot(kk2, categorySize="pvalue", foldChange=gene_list)
dev.off()

## Ridgeplot
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_KEGG_ridgeplot.pdf")
ridgeplot(kk2) + labs(x = "enrichment distribution")
dev.off()


# Use the `Gene Set` param for the index in the title, and as the value for geneSetId
pdf("experimental_vs_controlF_KEGG_gsea_plot.pdf")
gseaplot(kk2, by = "all", title = kk2$Description[1], geneSetID = 1)
dev.off()

library(pathview)

## Pick pathways to view plots for
## https://www.genome.jp/kegg/pathway.html

# Produce the native KEGG plot (PNG)
gga <- pathview(gene.data=kegg_gene_list, pathway.id="gga04260", species = kegg_organism)

# Produce a different plot (PDF) (not displayed here)
gga <- pathview(gene.data=kegg_gene_list, pathway.id="gga04260", species = kegg_organism, kegg.native = F)

knitr::include_graphics("gga04260.pathview.png")


# Produce the native KEGG plot (PNG)
gga <- pathview(gene.data=kegg_gene_list, pathway.id="gga04820", species = kegg_organism)

# Produce a different plot (PDF) (not displayed here)
gga <- pathview(gene.data=kegg_gene_list, pathway.id="gga04820", species = kegg_organism, kegg.native = T)

knitr::include_graphics("gga04820.pathview.png")




```


```{r}




### SECOND ATTEMPT
library(AnnotationHub) 
hub <- AnnotationHub()

query(hub, "Gallus")
Gallus <- hub[["AH116705"]]
Gallus


sample_gene <- sample(keys(Gallus), 100)
str(sample_gene)

library(clusterProfiler)
sample_test <- enrichGO(sample_gene, OrgDb=Gallus, pvalueCutoff=1, qvalueCutoff=1)
head(summary(sample_test))







```
